expected_effect: Keep the continuation shell available for true toxic extensions while reducing unnecessary symmetric fee spend in calmer paths.
expected_failure_signature: If the queue-aware trigger still fires on short noise, the branch recreates an over-spiky shell without enough extra edge.
expected_win_condition: Retain the raw anchor anti-arb profile while trimming broad fee drag enough to make the line promotable.
hidden_coupling_removed: The same event pulse should not both widen the shared floor and fully arm the continuation shell.
rationale: Spend continuation protection only when toxic flow stays aligned long enough to look like an adverse queue state, rather than paying a broad shell all the time.
title: Queue-aware continuation shell
why_not_coefficient_retune: This introduces a new continuation-budget trigger keyed to aligned toxic persistence instead of a stronger version of the old shell.
